worth_it_explanation: DV measurement is not uniform across channels. CTV and mobile in-app environments require publisher-side SDK integration to produce viewability and fraud signals; a campaign that buys inventory where that integration is absent gets impressions with no measurement behind them. Social platforms limit what third-party measurement can see, so DV's data on Meta, TikTok, and YouTube reflects only what those platforms expose, not a full independent count. These checks confirm that the channels generating the most spend are actually producing authenticated measurement, and that walled-garden coverage gaps are recorded rather than assumed away.
intro: DoubleVerify's measurement coverage depends on how each publisher or platform has integrated with DV's tagging infrastructure. Open-web display and video environments that accept a standard VAST or JavaScript tag are generally well covered. Connected TV (CTV) and mobile in-app environments require a publisher-side SDK integration, specifically one compatible with the IAB Tech Lab Open Measurement SDK (OM SDK), before viewability and fraud signals can be reported. Walled-garden social platforms expose only the measurement signals they choose to share through their own APIs, so DV's data on those platforms reflects a negotiated subset of signals rather than full independent measurement. These checks confirm that the channels and formats generating meaningful spend are covered by DV measurement, that CTV supply paths are OM SDK-integrated, and that social measurement limits are documented so post-campaign data is read accurately.

audit_checks.title: CTV campaigns run without confirming that DV measurement is active on each supply path
audit_checks.impact_rationale: CTV inventory bought across multiple supply paths, such as direct publisher deals and programmatic supply, may mix SDK-integrated and non-SDK-integrated placements. Impressions from non-integrated environments produce no viewability or fraud signal, so a portion of CTV spend runs without any third-party authentication while the overall campaign report makes it appear that DV is covering the full buy.

educate.base:
DV measurement on connected TV (CTV) depends on publisher-side integration. The industry standard for this integration is the IAB Tech Lab Open Measurement SDK (OM SDK), which enables third-party viewability and invalid traffic detection inside a CTV app without requiring a separate tag per measurement vendor. When a CTV publisher has integrated the OM SDK, DV can report viewability (whether the ad played on a screen that was powered on and actively in use) and fraud signals for that inventory. When a publisher has not integrated the OM SDK, those signals are unavailable and DV cannot authenticate the impressions from that environment.

A single CTV campaign often spans multiple supply paths: programmatic buys through a demand-side platform (DSP), direct publisher deals, and private marketplace (PMP) packages. Each supply path may use a different publisher app, and each app's OM SDK integration status is independent. A campaign might buy from five CTV apps, three of which are OM SDK-integrated and two of which are not. In the DV Pinnacle reporting dashboard, impressions from the non-integrated apps will either show no measurement data or appear under an 'unmeasured' or 'no data' classification, depending on how DV surfaces coverage gaps.

The practitioner cannot assume that buying CTV programmatically means DV is measuring it. DV's coverage depends on the specific apps in the supply path, not the buying method. A few signs this check applies:
  • The campaign's DV Pinnacle report shows CTV impressions with substantially lower measurement rates than the same campaign's open-web display impressions
  • The campaign buys CTV through a DSP or via direct deals but the team has not confirmed OM SDK integration status for the publishers involved
  • Post-campaign DV reports show a significant volume of CTV impressions with no viewability data attached
You may want to review the DV Pinnacle channel breakdown before a CTV campaign launches to confirm that the anticipated supply path has OM SDK coverage, rather than discovering the gap in post-campaign reporting.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running CTV campaigns to drive brand awareness before a sale period, unmeasured CTV impressions mean a portion of the campaign's brand safety and fraud protection is unconfirmed. You may want to confirm OM SDK integration for the CTV supply paths in the media plan before the campaign launches.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running CTV to reach a professional or household audience, DV fraud detection depends on the publisher having integrated the OM SDK. You may want to confirm coverage status for the specific CTV apps in the buy before the campaign begins.

learn_more.source_title: Open Measurement (OM) SDK: standards page covering CTV, mobile in-app, and web video integration
IAB Tech Lab Expands Open Measurement SDK to Connected TV

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify's per-publisher CTV integration status and its Pinnacle coverage reporting interface require DV Pinnacle access. Contact your DV account team for publisher-specific OM SDK integration status.

investigate.intro: In DV Pinnacle, review the channel breakdown for the CTV campaign to confirm what share of CTV impressions produced measurable viewability and fraud signals.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and open the campaign in question. Navigate to the reporting section and filter to the CTV channel or environment.

Review the impression volume alongside the 'Authentic Impression' rate or equivalent quality metric. Note whether a portion of CTV impressions show no viewability measurement or appear in an 'unmeasured' segment.
Compare the measured rate on CTV against the measured rate on open-web display for the same campaign. A substantially lower CTV measured rate indicates that some supply paths lack OM SDK integration.

If the campaign uses multiple CTV supply paths (e.g., a DSP buy plus a direct publisher deal), check whether the unmeasured impressions are concentrated in one supply path. That points to which publisher or deal lacks OM SDK integration.
Document the percentage of CTV impressions that are unmeasured and the supply path those impressions came through.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the share of CTV impressions with active DV measurement versus the share that are unmeasured. If more than 90 percent of CTV impressions produced viewability and fraud signals, the CTV coverage is considered adequate and the check passes. If 10 percent or more of CTV impressions are unmeasured, the check fails; the practitioner records the unmeasured percentage and the supply path responsible.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the percentage of CTV impressions with DV measurement versus unmeasured, and the supply path responsible for the unmeasured volume, to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that a portion of {{site_name}}'s CTV campaign impressions ran without active DoubleVerify measurement. Connected TV measurement depends on publisher-side OM SDK integration, and not all CTV supply paths in this campaign's media plan have that integration in place. Impressions from non-integrated environments produce no viewability or fraud signal, so that portion of the CTV buy is unverified. {{voice_recommend}} confirming OM SDK integration status for each CTV supply path before a campaign launches and reviewing the DV Pinnacle channel breakdown at the start of each flight to catch coverage gaps before significant budget is committed to unmeasured inventory.

audit_checks.title: Walled-garden social measurement constraints are not documented, so DV data is read as equivalent to open-web coverage
audit_checks.impact_rationale: DV's measurement inside Meta, TikTok, and YouTube depends on signals those platforms choose to share through their own APIs and integrations. When practitioners read DV social metrics without understanding those constraints, they may draw conclusions about brand safety or viewability quality that the underlying data does not support, or they may compare social and open-web DV metrics as if both reflect the same level of independent verification.

educate.base:
On the open web, DoubleVerify measures by inserting its own tag or SDK into the ad serving path. That gives DV direct access to impression signals without relying on a platform intermediary. On walled-garden social platforms (Meta, TikTok, YouTube, Snapchat, and similar), that direct access does not exist. Each platform controls what measurement signals it shares with third-party vendors, and DV receives only what the platform exposes through its official measurement API or partner integration.

In practice, this means that what DV can measure varies by platform and has changed over time as each platform has expanded or restricted its measurement APIs. On Meta, for example, DV's viewability measurement covers specific placements (such as Newsfeed) through Meta's own viewability reporting infrastructure, not through an independent tag. On TikTok, DV can measure viewability and brand suitability on placements where TikTok has granted the relevant API access. The measurement methodology for each platform is defined by an agreement between DV and the platform, and in some cases it has received Media Rating Council (MRC) accreditation to confirm the methodology meets industry standards.

The practical consequence is that a DV brand suitability or viewability score on a Meta campaign reflects what Meta's infrastructure allowed DV to see, not a fully independent audit. For some metrics and placements, the coverage is substantial. For others, it may be partial. Practitioners who compare DV viewability rates across Meta, TikTok, and open-web programmatic without accounting for these differences may draw conclusions that the data does not support. A few signs this check applies:
  • Post-campaign DV reports for social channels are compared directly to open-web DV reports without noting that measurement methodology differs by platform
  • The team is unaware of which social platforms have MRC-accredited DV measurement and which do not
  • Brand suitability or fraud metrics from a social campaign are used to draw conclusions about inventory quality without acknowledging that DV's access to those signals depends on the platform's cooperation
You may want to document, for each social platform in the media plan, which DV measurement signals are available and whether the methodology is MRC-accredited, so that post-campaign data is read with the correct context.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running paid social campaigns across Meta and TikTok alongside open-web programmatic, you may want to confirm that the team understands DV's social measurement reflects platform-shared signals rather than independent tagging before drawing comparisons between channel quality scores.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running brand suitability measurement through DV on LinkedIn and Meta, you may want to document which measurement signals DV can access on each platform so that brand suitability scores are read in their proper context.

learn_more.source_title: MRC digital accreditation listing, including DoubleVerify accreditations by environment and platform

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify's platform-specific measurement capabilities and the signals available on each social integration are documented in DV Pinnacle and in the DV partner support resources, which require DV account access. Contact your DV account team for current social coverage details.

investigate.intro: Review the DV Pinnacle channel breakdown for social campaigns and confirm whether the team has documented measurement methodology differences by platform.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and open the reporting view for a campaign that includes social placements. Filter by channel to view social metrics separately from open-web programmatic metrics.

Note which metric categories are populated for each social platform (e.g., viewability, brand suitability, fraud) and which are absent or show as 'not applicable'. This indicates which measurement signals DV can access on each platform.
Compare the available metric columns for a Meta or TikTok campaign against the columns for an open-web display campaign. Note differences in what is and is not measured.

Check whether any methodology notes or disclaimers appear in the DV Pinnacle interface for the social placements. DV sometimes surfaces notes indicating that a metric uses platform-reported data rather than independent measurement.
Ask the team whether the account has MRC-accredited DV measurement active for the specific social platforms in the plan, and document the answer.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each social platform in the media plan, which DV measurement signals are available and whether the team has documented that social measurement differs methodologically from open-web measurement. If the team has documented the constraints and is reading social DV data with that context, the check passes. If social and open-web DV metrics are compared as equivalent without any acknowledgment of methodology differences, the check fails; the practitioner records the platforms involved and the specific metrics being compared incorrectly.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record which social platforms are in the media plan, which DV metrics are available on each, and whether the team has documented measurement methodology differences, to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s social campaign DV data is being read without documentation of the measurement constraints that differ by platform. On walled-garden platforms such as Meta and TikTok, DoubleVerify measures only the signals those platforms expose through their measurement APIs, not through independent tagging. This means a DV viewability or brand suitability score on a social campaign reflects a platform-mediated measurement, not a fully independent one. {{voice_recommend}} documenting, for each social platform in the media plan, which DV measurement signals are available and whether the methodology carries MRC accreditation, so that post-campaign reporting applies the correct context when comparing social and open-web quality metrics.

audit_checks.title: DV Pinnacle channel and format breakdown is not reviewed, so channels with low or absent measurement go undetected
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A campaign that runs across display, video, CTV, and mobile in-app may have strong DV measurement on some channels and weak or absent measurement on others. If the overall campaign-level DV metrics are the only thing reviewed, channels with low coverage are averaged into the total without surfacing as a gap. Decisions about inventory quality are then based on an aggregate that misrepresents the coverage on the channels contributing the most spend.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify's DV Pinnacle dashboard surfaces measurement data at multiple levels of granularity: campaign, advertiser, channel, format, device, and placement. A practitioner who reviews only the campaign-level summary sees a blended average of all those environments. If a campaign spends 40 percent of its budget on CTV and the CTV supply path lacks OM SDK integration for a portion of the inventory, the campaign-level DV metrics will look reasonable because the well-measured display and video impressions dilute the unmeasured CTV impressions in the average.

A channel-level review surfaces those gaps. In DV Pinnacle, filtering to a specific channel or device environment shows the measurement rate, fraud rate, and viewability rate for that environment in isolation. A channel where most impressions are authenticated looks different from a channel where 30 percent of impressions produced no signal. That difference is only visible when the channel-level view is examined separately from the campaign total.

The IAB Tech Lab CTV Programmatic Guide documents measurement and verification standards for CTV environments, including how unified impression measurement and viewability standards apply across CTV devices. Reviewing DV channel data against those standards helps confirm whether the measurement infrastructure in place meets current industry expectations.

A few signs this check applies:
  • Post-campaign DV reviews are done at the campaign or advertiser level only, without filtering to individual channels or device environments
  • The campaign runs across three or more channels (e.g., open-web display, mobile in-app, and CTV) but the DV report reviewed does not break out metrics by channel
  • The team is unaware of the measurement rate difference between open-web and CTV or mobile in-app environments for this account
You may want to make a channel-level DV Pinnacle review a standard part of the campaign's in-flight and post-campaign quality process, rather than relying only on the campaign-level summary.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a cross-channel campaign across display, mobile in-app, and CTV, reviewing DV metrics at the channel level helps confirm that the environments contributing the most spend are also the best covered by DV measurement. You may want to pull the channel breakdown in DV Pinnacle at mid-flight to catch coverage gaps before the full budget is spent.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running mobile in-app and CTV alongside open-web programmatic, the mobile in-app measurement rate depends on which apps have integrated the OM SDK. You may want to review the DV Pinnacle channel breakdown to confirm mobile in-app measurement coverage before drawing conclusions about inventory quality on that channel.

learn_more.source_title: CTV Programmatic Guide: measurement and verification standards for connected TV advertising
Open Measurement (OM) SDK: standards for third-party measurement across mobile in-app, CTV, and web video

educate.learn_more_note: DV Pinnacle's channel-level filtering and coverage reporting interface require DV Pinnacle access. Contact your DV account team for guidance on setting up channel-specific reporting views.

investigate.intro: In DV Pinnacle, break the campaign's DV metrics out by channel and device environment to identify whether any channel has a lower measurement rate than others.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the campaign's reporting view. Locate the dimension or filter that segments impressions by channel or environment (e.g., Display, Video, CTV, Mobile In-App).

Apply the channel filter and review the Authentic Impression rate, viewability rate, and fraud rate for each channel separately. Record the measurement rate (the share of impressions that produced a DV signal) for each.
Identify any channel where the measurement rate is substantially lower than the others. A channel with more than 10 to 15 percent unmeasured impressions warrants investigation.

For any underperforming channel, check whether the impressions are concentrated in specific publishers, deals, or supply paths. This helps determine whether the gap is a publisher-level integration issue or a broader coverage gap.

Compare the overall campaign-level DV metrics against the channel-level breakdown to confirm how much the low-coverage channel is affecting the campaign total. Document the channel, the unmeasured impression percentage, and the supply path involved.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the DV measurement rate for each channel in the campaign. If all channels show more than 90 percent of impressions with active DV measurement, the check passes. If any channel shows 10 percent or more unmeasured impressions, the check fails; the practitioner records the channel name, the unmeasured percentage, and whether the gap is traceable to a specific publisher or supply path.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the DV measurement rate for each channel in the campaign above (Display, Video, CTV, Mobile In-App, Social) and note any channel with more than 10 percent unmeasured impressions to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DV quality reviews are conducted at the campaign level without a channel-by-channel breakdown. When campaigns run across display, video, connected TV (CTV), and mobile in-app simultaneously, low or absent DV measurement on one channel is absorbed into the campaign-level average and does not surface as a gap. {{voice_recommend}} making a channel-level DV Pinnacle review a standard step in both in-flight and post-campaign quality checks, filtering to each channel separately to confirm that the environments contributing the most spend are also producing the most complete DV measurement signal.
